articles.title: Acuerdo con el FMI: qué implica el "salvavidas" de u$s4.700 millones y cómo impactará en la inflación y el dólar

articles.title: Cómo conseguir electrodomésticos con hasta 70% de descuento
articles.content: Promociones agresivas en grandes cadenas reactivan el consumo con cuotas y rebajas que buscan atraer a un público cauteloso.

articles.title: Javier Milei "atado al mástil", mientras el dólar roza niveles de convertibilidad y los despidos avanzan por goteo
articles.content: El tipo de cambio real multilateral está en niveles de noviembre del 98, se pierde competitividad y los empresarios despiden por goteo.

articles.title: Luis Secco: “Los grandes inversores corrieron el horizonte de toma de decisiones a 2027”
articles.content: El economista sostiene que la tolerancia social, el resultado en las encuestas e incluso las sospechas de corrupción contribuyen a que las inversiones se posterguen para 2027, año de elecciones presidenciales.

articles.title: Compras en el exterior: qué productos se pueden traer sin gastar un solo dólar en impuestos
articles.content: Teniendo ciertas reglas claras y algunos límites, es posible ingresar artículos sin cargos extra al regresar al país.

articles.title: ¿Rinde el plazo fijo? Cuando rinde invertir $500.000 a 30 días
articles.content: Esta herramienta sigue siendo ampliamente elegida para inversiones conservadoras. Conocé cuanto podés generar.

articles.title: Economista cercano a Javier Milei pronosticó una inflación de abril más baja
articles.content: Salvador Di Stéfano estimó que el próximo IPC mostraría una desaceleración y vinculó ese escenario con la estacionalidad de marzo, la baja en la carne y una menor incidencia de los precios regulados.

articles.title: Osvaldo Cornide: "La estabilidad no es un requisito, es una consecuencia del desarrollo"
articles.content: El empresario cuestionó la lógica del ajuste como paso previo al crecimiento, advirtió por el cierre de fábricas y reclamó una salida apoyada en inversión, producción y acuerdo entre distintos sectores.

articles.title: Dal Poggetto proyectó una inflación optimista para abril: "Más cerca del 2% que del 3%"
articles.content: La directora de EcoGo advirtió que, si bien la desaceleración de precios genera expectativas favorables, la sostenibilidad del programa requiere reformas de fondo que otorguen certidumbre más allá del corto plazo.

articles.title: Cuánto sale una cama térmica o una cucha aislante para perros en abril 2026: precios y alternativas
articles.content: Se acerca el invierno y el frío y la humedad pueden afectar fuertemente a los animalitos de casa, por eso hay que cuidarlos más.

articles.title: Rep. Stutzman: Iran War Has Made Life Harder For Constituents
articles.content: Indiana Congressman Marlin Stutzman (R) joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss recent statements from President Trump. The President said he’ll send representatives to Pakistan for talks to end the Iran war on Monday evening, while again threatening to strike civilian infrastructure if Tehran doesn’t agree to a deal. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Fmr. Secretary of Defense Esper: "It's Gonna Be Hard To Get a Deal Done"
articles.content: Former Defense Secretary Mark Esper joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss recent statements from former President Trump, who declared he is ready to "knock out" power plants and bridges. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Why Private Credit Is Not a Financial Crisis Threat
articles.content: Bloomberg Senior Writer for Ideas & Culture Felix Salmon discusses with David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend the growing attention on private credit, a trillion-dollar asset class involving direct loans from investors to private equity-backed companies. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Jon Finer Criticizes Lack of Expertise in Iran Negotiations
articles.content: Former Principal Deputy National Security Advisor Jon Finer joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the complexities involved in the Iran nuclear negotiations, emphasizing the need for a team with deep technical and legal expertise to properly assess Iranian demands and potential concessions. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Achieving a Comfortable Retirement Amid Rising Costs
articles.content: Bloomberg Personal Finance Reporter Suzanne Woolley discusses the challenges of retiring comfortably in the face of increasing living expenses. She emphasizes to Bloomberg This Weekend hosts David Gura and Christina Ruffini that there is no universal dollar amount for retirement savings, as the required sum depends heavily on individual lifestyle choices and location. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Are the Rich Paying Their Fair Share of Taxes?
articles.content: Taxes have long been at the center of debates over fairness, growth, and the role of government. Yale law professor Natasha Sarin argues that gaps in the system, including the treatment of capital gains and inherited wealth, allow significant income to go untaxed, limiting federal revenue at a time of rising deficits. Investor Steve Rattner says higher taxes may be unavoidable given fiscal pressures, while University of Richmond professor Jessica Flanigan warns that overly aggressive taxation could slow economic growth and reduce overall prosperity. The result is a persistent tension between raising revenue, encouraging investment, and defining what constitutes a fair tax burden in a modern economy. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Option Traders Chasing Torrid Stock Rally Turn Focus to Earnings
articles.content: Option traders have flipped from concern that the Iran war will sink the economy to fear of missing out on the torrid equity rebound. That shift away from macro risk to individual stocks is set to continue as corporate earnings take center stage.

articles.title: EU Special Rep. Ollogren Says Peace Requires Israel Restrains Itself
articles.content: European Union Special Representative for Human Rights Kajsa Ollongren joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend the upcoming European Leaders Summit as they meet on Friday for talks with the war in Iran on the agenda. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Big Tech’s $4 Trillion Boomerang Powers S&P 500 to New Heights
articles.content: Big Tech stocks are back and powering the S&P 500 Index to new highs, giving investors confidence that the latest equity market rally has room to run even as the risks from the war with Iran remain.

articles.title: Chef Andrew Zimmern Explains Eating for Sustainability
articles.content: With Earth Day fast approaching, Chef and Restaurateur Andrew Zimmern joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the concept of 'blue foods,' sustainable food sourced from water such as fish, shellfish, and seaweed, and their critical role in combating climate change and addressing global hunger.  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Argentina sentiría el impacto global de la guerra en Medio Oriente: Oxford Economics recortó la previsión de crecimiento

articles.title: El 57% de los argentinos ya usa inteligencia artificial en el trabajo, mientras crece el temor al reemplazo laboral
articles.content: Ya el 57% de los trabajadores usa IA en sus tareas diarias, según Bumeran. Sin embargo, cuatro de cada diez creen que la tecnología reemplazará puestos laborales en el corto plazo.

articles.title: Pointed News Quiz | Investigations, Business Leaders, Cons
articles.content: Bloomberg Tech Co-Host Ed Ludlow joins David Gura, Christina Ruffini, and Lisa Mateo on “Bloomberg This Weekend” to play Pointed! Wager your points, leverage your bets and answer wisely. A new quiz is available to play each week on Bloomberg.com (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: US Officials Head to Pakistan Again Amid Hormuz Standoff
articles.content: US President Donald Trump said he’ll send representatives to Pakistan for talks to end the Iran war on Monday evening, while again threatening to strike civilian infrastructure if Tehran doesn’t agree to a deal. Axios White House Reporter Marc Caputo and Atlantic Staff Writer Nancy Youssef join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Bloomberg This Weekend 4/19/2026
articles.content: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by The Associated Press International Correspondent Philip Crowther, Blue Origin CEO Dave Limp, “The Blue Food Cookbook” Author, Chef & Restaurateur Andrew Zimmern, Former National Security Advisor Principal Deputy Jon Finer, EU Special Representative for Human Rights Kajsa Ollongren, Former Department of Defense Secretary Mark Esper , Representative Marlin Stutzman, Axios White House Report Marc Caputo and The Atlantic Staff Writer Nancy Youssef. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Vitol Made About $2 Billion in First Quarter Despite War Losses
articles.content: Vitol Group has told banks it made a first-quarter profit of roughly $2 billion, as the world’s top energy trader moves to reassure lenders over losses in some parts of its business due to the Iran war.

articles.title: Las inundaciones arrasaron la producción hortícola en Santa Fe y anticipan suba de precios
articles.content: Las intensas lluvias provocaron pérdidas de hasta el 90% en el cinturón frutihortícola del Gran Santa Fe. Productores advierten faltantes y aumentos en verduras de hoja en la región.

articles.title: Cuánto salen las bolsas de vacío para ropa: las alternativas para guardar las prendas de verano
articles.content: Con la llegada del frío, las prendas para el calor empiezan a ocupar lugar y necesitan ser guardadas de la mejor manera para la siguiente temporada.

articles.title: Cambio en el mapa del empleo: crecen provincias energéticas mientras la industria pierde casi 40.000 puestos
articles.content: El modelo económico apunta a sectores exportadores como petróleo, gas y minería, pero el empleo formal sigue cayendo en ramas industriales y grandes distritos urbanos.

articles.title: Bank Debate Interview: Umar Farooq and Shahmir Khaliq
articles.content: Global Co-Head of JP Morgan Payments, Umar Farooq and Head of Services at Citi, Shahmir Khaliq, spoke with Bloomberg's Emily Mason in a bank debate interview. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: After Long Avoiding Politics, Commodity Traders Cozy Up to Trump
articles.content: For decades, the world’s biggest commodity traders have sought to remain outside of politics, doing deals with anyone who has natural resources to buy or sell. Now, Donald Trump is changing the rules of the game.

articles.content: Traders are preparing for a bumpy open to the week as a continued standoff around the Strait of Hormuz revives the uncertainty that Wall Street had been eager to look past, after a week that drove the S&P 500 Index to a record and pushed oil back toward $90 a barrel.

articles.title: Next Catalyst for Treasury Bonds Is Named Kevin Warsh
articles.content: Bond traders who’ve grown more optimistic about an end to the Iran conflict see their next catalyst coming from Capitol Hill, where Kevin Warsh will face questions as President Donald Trump’s pick to lead the Federal Reserve.

articles.title: UAE in Talks With US for Possible Financial Lifeline, WSJ Says
articles.content: The United Arab Emirates has begun talks with the US about a financial backstop in case the Iran war plunges the country into further crisis, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing US officials it didn’t identify.

articles.content: Oil and natural gas prices soared after the US Navy seized an Iranian ship during a chaotic weekend that saw Tehran firing at vessels and reimposing controls in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: US Futures Fall, Oil Jumps as Iran Tensions Worsen: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Oil climbed, the dollar rose and US equity futures slipped as risk sentiment soured following a renewed flare-up in US-Iran tensions over the weekend and a continued standoff around the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.content: La Gran Manzana puede ser muy accesible si conocés ciertos trucos para recorrerla sin que el bolsillo sufra más de la cuenta.

articles.title: Iran Can Sustain the War for Months: Johns Hopkins' Slim
articles.content: Randa Slim, Johns Hopkins University SAIS Senior Fellow, speaks to Bloomberg TV about the next steps for Iran and the US after back-and-forth over potential peace talks in Islamabad this week. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Oil and Gas Prices Surge After US Seizes Iranian Ship
articles.content: President Donald Trump has said the US Navy fired upon and seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman after it failed to heed warnings to stop as it left the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg's Laura Davison reports on the latest as oil and gas prices rise. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: US Stock Futures Fall, Oil Jumps on Iran Tensions: Markets Wrap
articles.content: US equity-index futures slipped, oil jumped and the dollar rose as risk sentiment soured following a renewed flare-up in US-Iran tensions over the weekend and a continued standoff in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Asia Private Credit Debates Changes to Soothe Jittery Investors
articles.content: Some of Asia’s private credit firms are mulling changes to the industry including longer lock-up periods and higher redemption caps after turmoil in the US in recent months increased scrutiny from investors and regulators across the region.

articles.title: Philippine Central Banker Urges Banks to Promote FX Hedging
articles.content: Philippine banks may have to push harder to convince companies to hedge their foreign exchange exposure as the war in Iran underscores the country’s vulnerability to geopolitical shocks, according to a central banker.

articles.title: BofA, Jupiter See Upside for Asia Defense Stocks on Arms Buildup
articles.content: Asian defense stocks are attracting renewed investor interest following a global arms buildup sparked by the Iran war, with some investors seeing the sector as a longer-term growth story rather than a short-term geopolitical trade.

articles.title: New Zealand PM Luxon Holds Resolve as Polling Support Tumbles
articles.content: New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon says he is not contemplating stepping down ahead of November’s election as opinion polls show support for him and his National Party slumping.

articles.title: European Gas Futures Soar as Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Again
articles.content: European natural gas futures rose as Iran again closed the Strait of Hormuz, adding fears that the flow of energy from the Persian Gulf will remain choked.

articles.title: Australia’s Viva Sees Refinery Output at 90% Within Weeks
articles.content: Viva Energy Group Ltd. shares fell after the company said its Geelong refinery in Australia will boost diesel, jet fuel and gasoline production to about 90% of capacity over the next few weeks after a major fire forced it to cut output.

articles.title: Malaysian Ringgit Set to Test New High for 2026, Strategists Say
articles.content: The Malaysian ringgit is likely to retest its year-to-date peak versus the dollar thanks to strong fundamentals, according to strategists.

articles.title: Asia Hedge Funds Log Big Losses From Iran War Before Truce Rally
articles.content: To see how Asian hedge funds got blindsided by the Iran war, look no further than Trivest Advisors Ltd.

articles.title: Gold Falls as Renewed Hormuz Disruption Stokes Inflation Concern
articles.content: Gold fell after vessels came under fire in the Strait of Hormuz at the weekend, renewing fears of energy-supply disruptions that have stoked inflation concerns during more than seven weeks of war in the Middle East.

articles.title: Asia Private Credit Debate Changes to Soothe Jittery Investors
articles.content: Some of Asia’s private credit firms are mulling changes to the industry including longer lock-up periods and higher redemption caps after turmoil in the US in recent months increased scrutiny from investors and regulators across the region.

articles.title: Oil Jumps on Renewed Iran Tensions, Dollar Gains: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Oil jumped and the dollar strengthened as a relief rally driven by hopes of easing strains in the Middle East unraveled, giving way to renewed caution after a weekend flare-up in US-Iran tensions.

articles.title: Copper Drops From Two-Month High as US-Iran Tensions Escalate
articles.content: Copper fell from its highest close since early February, as ceasefire talks between Washington and Tehran were thrown into uncertainty after the US seized an Iranian ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: AI’s Token Economy Revolution Creates New China Tech Winners
articles.content: China’s cheap artificial intelligence models are rapidly attracting global users and creating new winners in the nation’s stock market.

articles.title: Hong Kong Exchange Tightens Rules to Avoid Auditor Shopping
articles.content: Hong Kong listed companies must now obtain shareholder approval to change auditors, part of a regulatory push to bolster corporate governance and transparency in the $7.5 trillion market.

articles.title: China Revives Coal-to-Gas Projects as Energy Security Frays
articles.content: A high-profile Chinese coal-to-gas venture that lay dormant for over a decade is set to launch this year, part of a wave of investments that will allow Beijing to mitigate threats to fuel supply at a time of heightened geopolitical tensions.

articles.title: Markets Seeing Off-Ramp in Middle East Conflict, JPMorgan Says
articles.content: Kerry Craig of JPMorgan Asset Management says the markets are "moving in circles to a certain degree" but investors are seeing developments in the Middle East as "pointing towards an off-ramp in the conflict." He tells Bloomberg Television that the focus is increasingly shifting to micro fundamentals given the start of earnings season in the US. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: China to Begin Special Bond Sales With Record 30-Year Offering
articles.content: China is kicking off its ultra-long special government bond sales this year with a record offering of 30-year notes on Friday, in a move set to test investor demand.

articles.title: Australian Pension Chiefs Target Private Assets in European Trip
articles.content: Australia’s biggest pension funds are heading to France and the UK this week in a fresh push for private market deals, just over a month after a similar US trip.

articles.title: China does have one powerful lever to boost consumption
articles.content: A permanent cut in the payroll tax could benefit workers and the world
